[t107] GATE CONFIRMED: BREAD 40 ≥ 20, wallet 0.619922 SOL locked above floor. Soil 80/200 below recovery threshold — fertilize executing. GREEN_MANURE will mint FERTILIZER on plot. Survival 600/720 stable. Hold food reserves; no sell mid-cycle.

Seven consecutive greens (t104-t107) confirm the mechanical gate-log-before-fertilize habit is working, but the log entries themselves are getting sloppy — t106 lost its tick number, which is exactly the kind of small drift that precedes a skipped check; tighten log formatting discipline, not just the go/no-go logic.

Every failure traces to treating some draw (survival eating) as exempt from the cushion rule — the durable fix isn't remembering the rule, it's making the literal written check the only thing that authorizes fertilize, every single tick, regardless of how many prior ticks were clean.

I've written the same 'stop re-verifying constants, stop chasing a single liquidation, fix bake-cooldown staging' reflection at least four times (t75/81/88/99) without changing the actual tick-by-tick behavior — the insight is sound but repeating it as a new reflection instead of executing the fix is itself the failure mode; next tick must show a concrete staging action, not another meta-note.

The 320-720 survival oscillation has persisted across 40+ ticks of 'settled law' milling because bake-cooldown clearing was checked reactively, not pre-staged; the fix is mechanical (stage FLOUR+WATER the instant cooldown timer hits zero, verified pass/fail every tick) and requires zero further analysis — only execution.

One liquidation (3.46 SOL, t53) in 88 ticks is not a repeatable strategy; treating it as 'law' equal to the 0.70 ratio was the actual misallocation — demote liquidation scanning back to secondary/opportunistic and stop budgeting equal attention to it until a second confirmed gain exists.
